law: Before starting, be sure to read to learn how to configure your environment to work with ClassKit, and to prepare to debug your ClassKit adoption.
In your implementation of the protocol’s method, you can use characteristics of your model objects to inform context creation.
If your app has contexts that can change, use this opportunity to reconfigure the context.
While contexts declare the structure of your app, you use instances to report progress through those contexts.
Model objects use their own identifier path to locate the matching context, and then use the context to manage activities.
Quizzes, once started, must be finished before the user can move to another task.
For this, you use activity items.

The method demonstrates the two ways in which you can register an activity item, either as primary or not: If you do decide to set a primary item, make sure you always set the same kind of primary item for a given activity.
For example, once you register the score item as the primary for a quiz activity, you must always use score this way.
